Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd

Facts.Text: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd is a company incorporated in the People's Republic of China.
Shida entered into a purchasing agreement with YZY for the supply of apples.
Shida supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80 to NTUC Fairprice.
YZY stopped making payments to Shida.
Purchasing agreement signed between Shida and YZY.
Shida issued invoices to YZY for the supply of apples.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Shida contracted with YZY or YY for the supply of apples to NTUC Fairprice.
Party_Details.Rule: A contract is formed when there is mutual consent and agreement on the terms between the parties.
Party_Details.Application: Shida argued that it had a valid contract with YZY, supported by purchasing agreements and invoices. YZY contended that the contract was with YY, not YZY.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the purchasing agreements, invoices, and bills of lading were authentic, establishing a contractual relationship between Shida and YZY.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore.
Hou Chao was a director and majority shareholder of YZY until 2019.
NTUC Fairprice began making payments directly to YZY's OCBC bank account.
YZY communicated to Shida that payments would be stopped.
YZY paid Shida US$8,829,148.20 for apples supplied.
YZY stated entered into a purchasing agreement with Shida for apple supply.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether YZY is legally responsible for the payment of apples supplied by Shida.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is bound by the terms of a contract if it is proven that the contract was entered into by an authorized representative.
Party_Details.Application: YZY argued that the contract was with YY and not YZY, and that payments were made on behalf of YY. Shida maintained that YZY was the contracting party.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that the evidence supported Shida's claim that YZY was the contracting party and owed the outstanding payment.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: YZY claimed to have overpaid Shida by US$466,994.53.
YZY stated that the purchasing agreement with Shida was not genuine.
YZY communicated its counterclaim against Shida for excess payments.
YZY stated that Mr Hou received RMB3,410,000 from Shida's associate.
YZY stated that RMB4,000,000 was transferred to Mr Hou's associate.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether YZY made excess payments to Shida and if so, whether these were due to a conspiracy involving Mr Hou.
Party_Details.Rule: A claim for excess payments requires proof of overpayment and a causal link to any stated misconduct.
Party_Details.Application: YZY argued that Mr Hou conspired with Shida to cause overpayments. Shida and Mr Hou denied any conspiracy or excess payments.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of excess payments or conspiracy, dismissing YZY's counterclaim.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd

Facts.Text: Shida entered into a purchasing agreement with YZY for the supply of apples.
Shida supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80 to NTUC Fairprice.
Shida communicated to YZY regarding outstanding payments.
Shida's associate transferred RMB3,410,000 to Mr Hou.
Shida's associate transferred RMB4,000,000 to Mr Hou's associate.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Shida was involved in a conspiracy with Mr Hou to cause YZY to make excess payments.
Party_Details.Rule: A party is legally responsible for conspiracy if it is proven that they acted in concert with another to cause harm through unlawful means.
Party_Details.Application: Shida denied any conspiracy or receipt of excess payments, arguing that all transactions were legitimate and accounted for.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of conspiracy or excess payments, ruling in favor of Shida.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Hou Chao

Facts.Text: Hou Chao was appointed as a director of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd (YZY).
Hou Chao acquired 60% of the share capital of YZY, becoming its majority shareholder.
Hou Chao received a transfer of RMB3,410,000 into his bank account from an individual named Mou.
A sum of RMB4,000,000 was transferred from Mou to an individual named Zhou, who later transferred RMB4,196,800 to Hou Chao.
Hou Chao was formally removed from his position as a director of YZY following an extraordinary general meeting.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a former director contravened management obligations by orchestrating excess corporate payments to a supplier in exchange for personal financial kickbacks.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act honestly and use reasonable diligence in the discharge of their duties, and must not authorize corporate payments that lack genuine commercial justification in order to secure a personal benefit.
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff by counterclaim stated that the director manipulated purchase prices and received kickbacks disguised as personal loans from the supplier's associates. The Defendant by counterclaim argued that the personal financial transfers were entirely unrelated to the company's apple purchasing business.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the plaintiff company failed to prove that any excess payments were actually made to the supplier. Furthermore, the court accepted the director's explanation that the personal fund transfers in China were related to an independent currency exchange arrangement, not a conspiracy to defraud the company. The counterclaim was dismissed.
